metadata.title: Design a 5-Day Spaced Study Schedule That Actually Works
metadata.note: CLI video in the REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(slate) · B05 CHANGE · B06 slate · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro.

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: Most students study one chapter at a time until they think they know it, then move to the next. The research calls this blocked practice. It produces high familiarity and low retention. Spaced practice — spreading sessions across multiple days with gaps — and interleaving — mixing chapters rather than blocking them — both produce stronger long-term retention. The problem is that designing a schedule that does both, across five chapters in five days, is harder than it looks. A CLI prompt can do it in thirty seconds.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'Blocked study = familiar + forgotten. Spaced + interleaved = remembered.' — no terminal yet

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Most students study one chapter at a time until they think they know it, then move to the next. The research calls this blocked practice. It produces high famil

beats.narration_text: One command. Ask Claude to design a five-day spaced study schedule for a biology exam covering chapters seven through eleven — nine hours total, interleaved chapters, at least one retrieval activity per session that is specific, not generic, and a two-hour mixed practice test on day five.

props.command: claude "Design a 5-day spaced study schedule for a biology exam covering chapters 7-11. Total: 9 hours across 5 days. Requirements: (1) interleave chapters (not blocked); (2) at least one retrieval activity per session — specific, not generic; (3) Day 5 is a 2-hour mixed practice test. Format as markdown calendar. Also output a per-session 6-step checklist."

beats.narration_text: The output is a markdown file — study schedule dot md. The key constraint is the interleaving requirement: no two consecutive sessions can cover the same chapter. The retrieval activities are specific — not 'review notes' but 'write the Krebs cycle from memory on a blank page' or 'draw the membrane structure without looking.' Generic activities are excluded.

props.code:
# study_schedule.md — 5-day spaced practice calendar output

| Day | Time  | Chapters    | Activity                                      |
|-----|-------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| 1   | 1.5hr | Ch7 + Ch9   | Read → blank-page recall of key terms (Ch7)   |
| 2   | 2.0hr | Ch8 + Ch10  | Concept map Ch8 → practice problems Ch10      |
| 3   | 1.5hr | Ch9 + Ch11  | Write Krebs cycle from memory (Ch9 retrieval) |
| 4   | 2.0hr | Ch7+Ch8+Ch9 | Interleaved flashcard drill across 3 chapters |
| 5   | 2.0hr | All Ch7-11  | Mixed practice exam — timed, no notes         |

## Per-session 6-step checklist
1. Set a timer before opening any material
2. Write what you already know about today's chapter (blank page)
3. Read the assigned section — annotate questions only
4. Close material — complete the retrieval activity
5. Check answers — mark gaps, not just errors
6. Log time and confidence (1-5) in study journal

beats.narration_text: The schedule lands. Five days, nine hours, five chapters — none of them blocked. Every session mixes at least two chapters, and every session has a retrieval activity named specifically enough that you know exactly what to do when you sit down.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of the markdown calendar table in terminal — Day 5 practice test row highlighted; per-session checklist visible below

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The schedule lands. Five days, nine hours, five chapters — none of them blocked. Every session mixes at least two chapters, and every session has a retrieval ac

beats.narration_text: Now adapt it. Ask Claude to modify the schedule for a student who has a two-hour class on Day 3 that cuts their study time in half — redistribute those lost sixty minutes across days two and four without breaking the interleaving rule.

props.command: claude "Modify the 5-day biology study schedule: Day 3 now has only 45 minutes available (class conflict). Redistribute the lost 45 minutes across Days 2 and 4. Keep the interleaving rule and specific retrieval activities. Show the revised schedule and note what changed."

beats.narration_text: The revised schedule moves thirty minutes each to days two and four, shrinks day three to a focused forty-five-minute retrieval-only session on the highest-priority chapter, and keeps day five intact. The total study time is preserved; the distribution adapts to the constraint.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of the revised calendar with Day 3 shortened and Days 2/4 extended — changes annotated; Day 5 practice test unchanged

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The revised schedule moves thirty minutes each to days two and four, shrinks day three to a focused forty-five-minute retrieval-only session on the highest-prio

beats.narration_text: A spaced, interleaved schedule is not harder to follow than a blocked one. It is harder to design. A CLI prompt removes the design friction — you get a schedule that applies two high-utility study techniques before you open a single textbook. The checklist enforces the retrieval step even when it feels like a bad idea.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Design friction removed — spaced + interleaved in 30 seconds'; checklist step 4 highlighted: 'Close material — complete the retrieval activity'

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A spaced, interleaved schedule is not harder to follow than a blocked one. It is harder to design. A CLI prompt removes the design friction — you get a schedule

beats.narration_text: Your move: run this prompt for your next exam — with your actual chapter numbers, your actual available hours, and your actual conflicts. The two constraints that matter are: interleave every session and name the retrieval activity specifically enough that future-you knows exactly what to do.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) run prompt with your actual chapters + hours, 2) check: are sessions interleaved?, 3) check: is retrieval activity named specifically?
